text.title: csharp-xunit-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for csharp-xunit-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: WebApplicationFactory<Program> test fails with "The Program class was not found" or "Entry point not found" error.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Program.cs does not have `public partial class Program;` at the bottom. The test host cannot discover the entry point. Add this line to Program.cs and retry.

failure-mode.description: Initializing expensive dependencies (HttpClient, DbContext) in the test class constructor instead of IClassFixture or IAsyncLifetime.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: xUnit creates a new test class instance per test method. Constructor init is called N times, exhausting resources. Use IClassFixture to share setup across methods.

failure-mode.description: Test methods returning void instead of Task for async tests.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Exceptions in async void methods are unobserved; the test "passes" and the error is lost. Always return Task or Task<T> for async tests.

failure-mode.description: Async test methods that return void instead of Task.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Exceptions in async void methods are unobserved; the test "passes" and the error is lost. xUnit doesn't warn for test methods (unlike regular methods). Always return Task or Task<T>.

failure-mode.description: Initializing expensive resources (HttpClient, DbContext) in the test class constructor instead of IClassFixture or IAsyncLifetime.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: xUnit creates a new test class instance per test method by design. Constructor init is called N times, exhausting resources. Agents pay tens of milliseconds per test. Use IClassFixture to share setup across methods.

failure-mode.description: WebApplicationFactory<Program> test fails with cryptic "entry point not found" error.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Program.cs is missing `public partial class Program;` at the bottom. The test host cannot discover the entry point. Add this line and retry.

failure-mode.description: Moq Setup chains with non-matching argument matchers — e.g., Setup(m => m.Method(1)) but the test calls m.Method(2).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Moq silently returns default when matchers don't match, leading to false-positive null asserts in the test. Use It.Is<T>(...) for precise matching, or strict mocks to catch mismatches.

failure-mode.description: Parallel test execution collisions: agents reuse a static Faker, shared HttpClient, or mutable cache.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Shared static state persists across tests and causes flakes in parallel runs. Make shared state per-instance, or disable parallelization with [CollectionDefinition(DisableParallelization = true)] only when necessary.

failure-mode.description: Tests marked [Fact(Skip = "TODO...")] to suppress failures, not to be fixed later.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Skipped tests rot. CI should fail on skipped tests by default. Reject any Skip pragmatism in code review.

failure-mode.description: Using UseInMemoryDatabase for integration tests that depend on relational constraints (foreign keys, unique indexes).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: In-memory DBs skip relational constraints; tests pass that would fail in production Postgres/SQL Server. Use SQLite-in-memory (via WebApplicationFactory override) or Testcontainers for reality-based testing.
